feature_completeness_summary: The merged branch satisfies the implementation, resize-seam, and documentation acceptance criteria for the lower-panel layout fix, but it still lacks the plan-requested live GUI large-ship split-layout runtime case, so feature delivery is conditionally complete rather than fully demonstrated end-to-end.

findings.summary: Live GUI coverage still exercises only a Destroyer/Frigate narrow-width case and does not prove the originally risky large-ship split-layout scenario at runtime.

Implementation behavior, resize seam preservation, and updated documentation align with the plan and reviewed branch state; no broader feature-level defects were confirmed beyond the remaining live-coverage gap.

follow_up_feature_requests: Add a tactical live-GUI regression that selects a heavy cruiser or battleship at a split-eligible width and asserts the full ship-stats block remains visible without right-edge clipping, while keeping the narrow-width stacked fallback checks.
assumptions: The final review target is the current HEAD shared by branches layout-update-reviewer-20260516 and layout-update, as indicated by git log in the assigned worktree.

The feature plan's implementation and regression-strengthening expectations can be evaluated from the single delivered subtask artifact chain because the reviewed code, tests, docs, and verifier outputs cover both concerns together.
